beak.utilities.vector_processing module

beak.utilities.vector_processing._get_geometry_type(src: GeoDataFrame) → str[source]

Determine the geometry type of a GeoDataFrame.

Checks the geometry type of all geometries in the provided GeoDataFrame and returns a string indicating the type of geometry. It supports ‘Point’, ‘MultiPoint’, ‘LineString’, ‘MultiLineString’, ‘Polygon’, and ‘MultiPolygon’.

Parameters:

src – The GeoDataFrame whose geometry type is to be determined.

Returns:

A string indicating the geometry type of the GeoDataFrame.

Return type:

geometry_type

Raises:

ValueError – If the GeoDataFrame contains mixed or unsupported geometry types.

beak.utilities.vector_processing._rasterize_features(src: Tuple[GeometryArray, ndarray], template: Tuple[ndarray, dict], custom_fill_value: int | float | None = None, custom_nodata_value: int | float | None = None) → Tuple[ndarray, dict][source]

Rasterize geometries into a raster array based on a given template.

Parameters:

  • src – A tuple containing geometries and values. - GeometryArray: An array of geometries to be rasterized. - np.ndarray: An array of values corresponding to the geometries.

  • template – A tuple containing the template raster array and its metadata. - np.ndarray: The template raster data array. - Dict: The template raster metadata.

  • custom_fill_value – A custom fill value for pixels without geometries.

  • custom_nodata_value – A custom nodata value for the raster. If not provided, the nodata value will be set to the minimum of the data type.

Returns:

  • out_array: The rasterized array.

  • out_meta: The metadata dictionary for the rasterized array.

Return type:

A tuple containing

beak.utilities.vector_processing._transform_from_geometries(src: GeoDataFrame, resolution: int | float) → Tuple[int, int, Affine][source]

Calculate the transform parameters required to convert the input geometries to a specified resolution.

Parameters:

  • src – The input GeoDataFrame containing the geometries.

  • resolution – The desired resolution for the transformation.

Returns:

A tuple containing the width, height, and transform

parameters required for the transformation.

Return type:

Tuple[float, float, transform.Affine]

beak.utilities.vector_processing.create_geodataframe_from_points(src: DataFrame, coord_columns: Tuple[str, str] = ('longitude', 'latitude'), crs: str | CRS | CRS | None = None) → GeoDataFrame[source]

Creates a GeoDataFrame from a DataFrame containing geographic coordinates.

Parameters:

  • src – The input DataFrame containing geographic coordinates.

  • coord_columns – A tuple of strings representing the names of the longitude and latitude columns.

  • crs – An optional CRS string or rasterio CRS object for projecting the GeoDataFrame.

Returns:

The resulting GeoDataFrame.

Return type:

gpd.GeoDataFrame

beak.utilities.vector_processing.extract_values_from_points(src_points: DataFrame, src_arrays: List[ndarray], src_descriptions: List[str], template: Tuple[ndarray, Dict]) → DataFrame[source]

Extract values from a raster array at specified point locations.

Parameters:

  • src_points – A GeoDataFrame containing point geometries and attributes.

  • src_arrays – A list of raster arrays to extract values from.

  • src_descriptions – A list of names for the extracted values.

  • template – A tuple containing the template raster array and its metadata. - np.ndarray: The template raster data array. - dict: The template raster metadata.

Returns:

A DataFrame containing the extracted values and additional attributes from the input GeoDataFrame.

beak.utilities.vector_processing.prepare_geodataframe(src: GeoDataFrame, template: Tuple[ndarray, dict], query: str | None = None) → GeoDataFrame[source]

Prepare a GeoDataFrame for rasterization.

Ensure that the input GeoDataFrame is not empty, optionally filters it based on a query, and reproject’s it to the coordinate reference system (CRS) of the provided template raster.

Parameters:

  • src – The input GeoDataFrame to be prepared for rasterization.

  • template – A tuple containing the template array and its metadata. - template_array: The array representing the template raster. - template_meta: The metadata dictionary for the template raster.

  • query – An optional query string to filter the GeoDataFrame.

Returns:

The queried and reprojected GeoDataFrame

Return type:

gpd.GeoDataFrame

Raises:

  • AssertionError – If the input GeoDataFrame is empty.

  • AssertionError – If the queried GeoDataFrame is empty.

beak.utilities.vector_processing.rasterize_binary(src: GeoDataFrame, template: Tuple[ndarray, dict], custom_fill_value: int | float | None = None, custom_nodata_value: int | float | None = None) → Tuple[ndarray, dict][source]

Convert features into a binary raster.

Use cases:

  • Template (coverage) creation: use custom_fill_value of None.

  • Encoding categorical features: use custom_fill_value of 0.

  • Label creation: use custom_fill_value of 0.

Parameters:

  • src – The input GeoDataFrame containing geometries to rasterize.

  • template – A tuple containing the template array and its metadata.

  • custom_fill_value – Custom fill value for the raster.

  • custom_nodata_value – Custom nodata value for the raster. If not provided, the nodata value will be set to the minimum of the data type.

Returns:

A tuple containing the rasterized array and its metadata.

Return type:

Tuple[np.ndarray, dict]

Raises:

AssertionError – If CRS mismatch between source and template.

beak.utilities.vector_processing.rasterize_continuous(src: GeoDataFrame, template: Tuple[ndarray, dict], column_to_raster: str, custom_nodata_value: int | float | None = None) → Tuple[ndarray, dict][source]

Rasterize continuous values from a specified column in a GeoDataFrame.

Takes a GeoDataFrame and a specified column containing continuous data, and rasterizes the values into a raster array based on a given template.

Parameters:

  • src – The input GeoDataFrame containing geometries and continuous data.

  • template – A tuple containing the template raster array and its metadata. - np.ndarray: The template raster data array. - dict: The template raster metadata.

  • column_to_raster – The name of the column in the GeoDataFrame to rasterize.

  • custom_nodata_value – Custom nodata value for the raster. If not provided, the nodata value will be set to the minimum of the data type.

Returns:

A tuple containing:

  • np.ndarray: The rasterized array.

  • dict: The metadata dictionary for the rasterized array.

Return type:

Tuple[np.ndarray, dict]

beak.utilities.vector_processing.rasterize_encode_categorical(src: GeoDataFrame, template: Tuple[ndarray, dict], column_to_raster: str, custom_nodata_value: int | float | None = None) → List[Tuple[str, ndarray, dict]][source]

Rasterize a categorical column from a GeoDataFrame into multiple binary rasters.

Takes a GeoDataFrame and a specified column containing categorical data, and rasterizes each unique value in that column into a separate binary raster indicating presence (1) or absence (0) of that value in the original geometries.

Parameters:

  • src – The input GeoDataFrame containing geometries and categorical data.

  • template – A tuple containing the template array and its metadata.

  • column_to_raster – The name of the column in the GeoDataFrame to rasterize.

  • custom_nodata_value – Custom nodata value for the raster. If not provided, the nodata value will be set to the minimum of the data type.

Returns:

A list of tuples, each containing:

  • The name of the raster (str) in the format “column_name_value”.

  • The rasterized array (np.ndarray).

  • The metadata dictionary (dict) for the raster.

Return type:

out_encodings
